title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
pythonDriver:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().split('\n')
nums = list(map(int, data[0].split()))
target = int(data[1])

{{USER_CODE}}

result = twoSum(nums, target)
print(result[0], result[1])

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list. Input is space separated values, -1 means null.
pythonDriver:
import sys

class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

def buildList(vals):
    if not vals: return None
    head = ListNode(vals[0])
    cur = head
    for v in vals[1:]:
        cur.next = ListNode(v)
        cur = cur.next
    return head

def printList(head):
    res = []
    while head:
        res.append(str(head.val))
        head = head.next
    print(' '.join(res))

{{USER_CODE}}

data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().split()))
head = buildList(data)
result = reverseList(head)
printList(result)

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a sorted list. Input has two lines each with space separated values.
pythonDriver:
import sys

class ListNode:
    def __init__(self,val=0,next=None):
        self.val=val;self.next=next

def build(vals):
    if not vals:return None
    h=ListNode(vals[0]);c=h
    for v in vals[1:]:c.next=ListNode(v);c=c.next
    return h

def printList(h):
    r=[]
    while h:r.append(str(h.val));h=h.next
    print(' '.join(r))

{{USER_CODE}}

lines=sys.stdin.read().split('\n')
l1=build(list(map(int,lines[0].split())) if lines[0].strip() else [])
l2=build(list(map(int,lines[1].split())) if len(lines)>1 and lines[1].strip() else [])
printList(mergeTwoLists(l1,l2))
